name: Creamy Tomato Soup in a Bread Bowl
headline: Dough-liciously easy. | 2-3 Servings
description:
There’s nothing more comforting than a warm, hearty bowl of soup–especially when that bowl is actually warm, crusty sourdough bread! Not just any soup will do, though—this rich and creamy tomato soup happens to be extra special. It’s made with a base of flavorful vegetable broth and sweet cream, and it’s chock-full of a medley of tomatoes, garlic, and spices. We wouldn’t leave you hanging without something to dunk into your soup, either: Save the bread you scoop out of the bowl for dipping! The best part? All you have to do is heat it up and dig right in! One slurp will have you (bread) bowled over.
Nutrition values are representative of a 1/2 bundle serving

steps.instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Place bag in pot; cook for 20 minutes, then carefully remove. (Caution: Bag will be very hot.) Cut one corner with scissors before serving.

MICROWAVE ALTERNATIVE: Cut one corner of the bag; pour soup into a microwave-safe container. (Do not microwave in cup.) Cover loosely with microwave-safe plastic wrap or a paper towel. Microwave on high, stirring halfway through, 5-6 minutes. (Caution: Container and soup will be very hot.)

BREAD BOWL: Adjust rack to middle position and preheat oven to 350 degrees. Cut a 2-4-inch circle into the top of the loaf and remove. Scoop out the inside of the bread to create a bowl. Brush inside of bread bowl with 1 TBSP olive oil or melted butter; bake on middle rack until lightly toasted and warmed through, 7-8 minutes.
